---
title-block: false
---

::: {.home-page}
::: {.intro-block}
::: {.intro-row}
::: {.intro-left}
![](yihuang_fig/yihuang_profile.jpeg){fig-alt="Profile photo of Yi Huang" width="100%" fig-align="left"}
:::

::: {.intro-right}
**Incoming Assistant Professor**\
[Min H. Kao Department of EECS](https://eecs.utk.edu/)\
[Tickle College of Engineering](https://tickle.utk.edu/)\
[Foundation AI Cluster](https://research.utk.edu/cluster-hire/foundational-artificial-intelligence/)\
[University of Tennessee, Knoxville](https://www.utk.edu/)
:::

::: {.intro-extra}
Email: [yihuang\@utk.edu](mailto:yihuang@utk.edu)

Address: 1520 Middle Drive, Knoxville, TN 37996-2250

[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Y0JqxgwAAAAJ&hl=en) &bull; [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9628-1721) &bull; [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yi-huang-b046a91aa/?skipRedirect=true)
:::
:::
:::

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const row = document.querySelector(".intro-row");
  const textCols = row
    ? Array.from(row.querySelectorAll(".intro-right, .intro-extra"))
    : [];
  const img = row?.querySelector(".intro-left img");
  const nameEl = row?.querySelector(".intro-left .intro-name");
  if (!row || !img || textCols.length === 0) return;

  const mobileMedia = window.matchMedia("(max-width: 900px)");

  function syncIntroPhotoHeight() {
    if (mobileMedia.matches) {
      textCols.forEach((col) => {
        col.style.marginTop = "0";
      });
      img.style.height = "auto";
      return;
    }

    const nameOffset = nameEl
      ? nameEl.getBoundingClientRect().height +
        parseFloat(window.getComputedStyle(nameEl).marginBottom || "0")
      : 0;
    textCols.forEach((col) => {
      col.style.marginTop = `${Math.max(0, Math.round(nameOffset))}px`;
    });

    img.style.height = "auto";
    const targetHeight = textCols.reduce((maxHeight, col) => {
      return Math.max(maxHeight, col.getBoundingClientRect().height);
    }, 0);
    if (targetHeight > 0) {
      img.style.height = `${Math.round(targetHeight)}px`;
    }
  }

  syncIntroPhotoHeight();
  window.addEventListener("resize", syncIntroPhotoHeight);
  if (mobileMedia.addEventListener) {
    mobileMedia.addEventListener("change", syncIntroPhotoHeight);
  } else {
    mobileMedia.addListener(syncIntroPhotoHeight);
  }
});
</script>

## Research Vision

### The Challenge: The Gap Between AI and Human Intelligence
Artificial intelligence (AI) has become a part of our everyday life, powering everything from voice assistants to medical diagnostics. But this progress comes at the cost of unsustainable scaling in data and energy consumption. A huge gap persists between AI and human intelligence, particularly in energy efficiency, where data centers behind AI consume as much electricity as small cities while the human brain operates on the energy of a single meal, and in cognitive capability, where AI requires massive datasets to learn tasks that humans can master from limited experience. 


### The Goal: Human-level Energy Efficiency and Cognitive Capability  
Our research vision is to close this gap through a hardware-software co-design paradigm that spans multiple layers of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) and draws inspiration from cognitive neuroscience. The ultimate goal is to approach the energy efficiency and cognitive capabilities of the human brain, enabling next-generation AI systems across a broad range of applications.

<div class="svg-responsive svg-overlay">
  <img src="work_fig/research_vision.svg" alt="research vision" />
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-1" href="publications.html#ref-Ye2023" data-ref-id="ref-Ye2023" aria-label="Reference: Ye2023">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-2" href="publications.html#ref-Yang2020" data-ref-id="ref-Yang2020" aria-label="Reference: Yang2020">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-3" href="publications.html#ref-Shi2018a" data-ref-id="ref-Shi2018a" aria-label="Reference: Shi2018a">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-4" href="publications.html#ref-Yang2018b" data-ref-id="ref-Yang2018b" aria-label="Reference: Yang2018b">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-5" href="publications.html#ref-Nowshin2024" data-ref-id="ref-Nowshin2024" aria-label="Reference: Nowshin2024">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-6" href="publications.html#ref-Liu2022" data-ref-id="ref-Liu2022" aria-label="Reference: Liu2022">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-7" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2018a" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2018a" aria-label="Reference: Huang2018a">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-8" href="publications.html#ref-Ravichandran2026" data-ref-id="ref-Ravichandran2026" aria-label="Reference: Ravichandran2026">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-9" href="publications.html#ref-Jiang2025" data-ref-id="ref-Jiang2025" aria-label="Reference: Jiang2025">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-10" href="publications.html#ref-huang2024hardware" data-ref-id="ref-huang2024hardware" aria-label="Reference: Huang2024hardware">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-11" href="publications.html#ref-Zhao2026" data-ref-id="ref-Zhao2026" aria-label="Reference: Zhao2026">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-12" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2025" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2025" aria-label="Reference: Huang2025">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-13" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2024" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2024" aria-label="Reference: Huang2024">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-14" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2023_APL" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2023_APL" aria-label="Reference: Huang2023_APL">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-15" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2023_Nano" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2023_Nano" aria-label="Reference: Huang2023_Nano">[...]</a>
</div>

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const refLinks = document.querySelectorAll(".svg-ref[data-ref-id]");
  if (!refLinks.length) return;
  const compactMedia = window.matchMedia("(max-width: 768px)");

  function renderLabels() {
    refLinks.forEach((link) => {
      const num = link.dataset.refNum;
      if (!num) return;
      link.textContent = compactMedia.matches ? num : `[${num}]`;
    });
  }

  fetch("publications.html")
    .then((r) => r.text())
    .then((html) => {
      const parser = new DOMParser();
      const doc = parser.parseFromString(html, "text/html");
      const refs = Array.from(doc.querySelectorAll("#refs .csl-entry"));
      if (!refs.length) return;
      const indexMap = new Map(refs.map((el, i) => [el.id, i + 1]));
      refLinks.forEach((link) => {
        const id = link.getAttribute("data-ref-id");
        const num = indexMap.get(id);
        if (num) {
          link.dataset.refNum = String(num);
          link.setAttribute("aria-label", `Reference ${num}`);
        }
      });
      renderLabels();
      if (compactMedia.addEventListener) {
        compactMedia.addEventListener("change", renderLabels);
      } else {
        compactMedia.addListener(renderLabels);
      }
    })
    .catch(() => {
      // If fetch fails (e.g. local file without server), keep placeholder.
    });
});
</script>


### The Approach: A Cross-layer Co-design Framework 
We pursue this vision through analog in-memory computing (AIMC) systems inspired by the human brain and built upon emerging memory devices. This approach emulates the operating principles of biological neural systems: data is processed where it is stored, eliminating the separation between CPU and memory that characterizes conventional architectures, and computation operates directly on analog signals, without discrete digital data representations.

Building on our prior work (see figure above), our approach centers on a cross-layer co-design framework that spans devices, circuits, architectures, algorithms, and applications:  

::: {.h4-card-grid}

::: {.h4-card}
<div class="h4-text-title">Emerging memory devices</div>
We model emerging memory devices, including non-volatile and volatile memristors, to establish the physical foundation for AIMC hardware that exploits their rich analog dynamics.

::: {.h4-image-placeholder}
![](work_fig/device_huang.svg){fig-alt="Device image"}
:::
:::

::: {.h4-card}
<div class="h4-text-title">Synapse and neuron circuits</div>
We design analog and mixed-signal circuits that leverage these device-level dynamics to emulate the stochastic and temporal behavior of biological synapses and neurons.

::: {.h4-image-placeholder}
![](work_fig/synapse_htm_liu.svg){fig-alt="Circuit image"}
:::
:::

::: {.h4-card}
<div class="h4-text-title">Brain-inspired architectures</div>
We develop hierarchical, distributed architectures with efficient on-chip communication across analog and digital domains, enabling multilayer information processing and highly parallel computation.

::: {.h4-image-placeholder}
![](work_fig/architect_htm_liu.svg){fig-alt="Architecture image"}
:::
:::

::: {.h4-card}
<div class="h4-text-title">Algorithm co-design</div>
We co-design neural network structures and on-chip learning algorithms optimized for brain-inspired hardware substrates, incorporating spike-based, event-driven, and analog features to enable real-time on-chip learning.

::: {.h4-image-placeholder}
![](work_fig/algorithm_hdc_huang.svg){fig-alt="Algorithm image"}
:::
:::

::: {.h4-card}
<div class="h4-text-title">Edge applications</div>
We integrate these energy-efficient, low-latency systems into sensors, wearables, and mobile devices to address a wide range of edge applications that require instantaneous intelligent decisions with minimal power.

::: {.h4-image-placeholder}
![](work_fig/application_huang.svg){fig-alt="Application image"}
:::
:::

::: {.h4-card}
<div class="h4-text-title">Energy-efficient AI systems</div>
We build energy-efficient and intelligent AI systems by bridging AIMC hardware with brain-inspired architectures and algorithms, harnessing the hardware-software co-design for the next-generation AI.

::: {.h4-image-placeholder}
![](work_fig/system_img.svg){fig-alt="System image"}
:::
:::

:::
